ddls:
CREATE TABLE "cohorts" (
  "cohort_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each generational cohort */,
  "cohort_name" TEXT /* Name of the generational cohort */,
  "cohort_start_year" INTEGER /* Starting year for the generational cohort */,
  "cohort_end_year" INTEGER /* Ending year for the generational cohort */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cohort_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shopping_frequencies" (
  "frequency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each shopping frequency category */,
  "frequency_name" TEXT /* Name of the shopping frequency category */,
  "frequency_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the frequency category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("frequency_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "survey_responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each survey response */,
  "cohort_id" INTEGER /* ID of the generational cohort the respondent belongs to */,
  "frequency_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shopping frequency category the respondent selected */,
  "response_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of respondents who selected this frequency */,
  "sample_size" INTEGER /* Sample size for this frequency category */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date when the survey response was recorded */,
  "response_method" TEXT /* Method through which the response was collected (e.g., online, phone, in-person) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_frequency_id FOREIGN KEY ("frequency_id") REFERENCES shopping_frequencies ("frequency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_cohort_id FOREIGN KEY ("cohort_id") REFERENCES cohorts ("cohort_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "respondents" (
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each respondent */,
  "cohort_id" INTEGER /* ID of the generational cohort the respondent belongs to */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the respondent */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the respondent */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the respondent */,
  "income_range" TEXT /* Income range of the respondent */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Highest level of education completed by the respondent */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the respondent */,
  "employment_status" TEXT /* Employment status of the respondent (e.g., employed, unemployed, student) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("respondent_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_respondents_cohort_id FOREIGN KEY ("cohort_id") REFERENCES cohorts ("cohort_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shopping_channels" (
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each shopping channel */,
  "channel_name" TEXT /* Name of the shopping channel */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the shopping channel */,
  "channel_type" TEXT /* Type of shopping channel (e.g., e-commerce, brick-and-mortar) */,
  "channel_popularity" INTEGER /* Metric indicating the popularity of the channel */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "channel_preferences" (
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each channel preference */,
  "cohort_id" INTEGER /* ID of the generational cohort */,
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shopping channel */,
  "preference_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of the cohort that prefers this channel */,
  "preference_reason" TEXT /* Reason why the cohort prefers this channel */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("preference_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_channel_preferences_cohort_id FOREIGN KEY ("cohort_id") REFERENCES cohorts ("cohort_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_channel_preferences_channel_id FOREIGN KEY ("channel_id") REFERENCES shopping_channels ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transactions" (
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transaction */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent who made the transaction */,
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shopping channel used for the transaction */,
  "amount" REAL /* Amount spent in the transaction */,
  "transaction_date" TEXT /* Date when the transaction occurred */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Method of payment used in the transaction (e.g., credit card, cash) */,
  "item_category" TEXT /* Category of items purchased (e.g., electronics, clothing) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transactions_channel_id FOREIGN KEY ("channel_id") REFERENCES shopping_channels ("channel_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transactions_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "market_research" (
  "research_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each market research data point */,
  "cohort_id" INTEGER /* ID of the generational cohort the data pertains to */,
  "data_point" TEXT /* Description of the data point */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the data point */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the market research data */,
  "research_date" TEXT /* Date when the market research data was collected */,
  "research_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the market research data (e.g., survey, interview) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("research_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_market_research_cohort_id FOREIGN KEY ("cohort_id") REFERENCES cohorts ("cohort_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "cohort_id" INTEGER /* ID of the generational cohort the prediction is for */,
  "future_trend" TEXT /* Description of the predicted trend */,
  "likelihood" REAL /* Likelihood of the trend occurring */,
  "predicted_date" TEXT /* Date when the prediction was made */,
  "predictive_model" TEXT /* Model or algorithm used to make the prediction */,
  "confidence_interval" TEXT /* Confidence interval for the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_cohort_id FOREIGN KEY ("cohort_id") REFERENCES cohorts ("cohort_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "cohort_id" INTEGER /* ID of the generational cohort the report is about */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the report */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the report */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author who created the report */,
  "publication_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was published */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., trend analysis, market forecast) */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, HTML) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_cohort_id FOREIGN KEY ("cohort_id") REFERENCES cohorts ("cohort_id")
)
